content:
Key Findings

  • Rossendale Borough Council's Customer Services & E-Government department spent £20.0M over the five financial years analysed.
  • This department accounts for 29.8% of the council's total analysed spending.
  • Customer Services & E-Government is the largest spending department within Rossendale Borough Council.
  • The council's total analysed spending across all departments amounted to £67.3M.

Understanding Rossendale's Investment in Customer Services & E-Government

Rossendale Borough Council has allocated a substantial sum to its Customer Services & E-Government department. Over the five financial years from 2021/22 to 2025/26, this department's spending reached £20.0M. This significant investment highlights the council's focus on modernising its public interactions and digital infrastructure. The department is crucial for residents accessing council services efficiently, whether through online portals, contact centres, or other digital channels.

The £20.0M spent by Customer Services & E-Government represents a considerable portion of the council's overall budget. Specifically, this department accounts for 29.8% of the total £67.3M analysed spending for Rossendale Borough Council. This proportion indicates a strategic priority placed on ensuring accessible and effective customer service delivery, alongside the development of e-government solutions. Such an allocation supports the ongoing shift towards digital-first approaches in local government, aiming to streamline processes and improve user experience.

The investment in Customer Services & E-Government is vital for the council's operational efficiency and its ability to meet resident expectations. By focusing resources on this area, Rossendale Borough Council aims to enhance how it communicates with the public and delivers essential services. This includes maintaining digital platforms, managing customer enquiries, and implementing technology solutions that benefit the community. The consistent spending over a five-year period suggests a sustained commitment to these objectives.

Customer Services & E-Government as a Leading Spender

An analysis of Rossendale Borough Council's spending across all departments reveals that Customer Services & E-Government is the highest spending area. The council's total analysed expenditure across all services amounted to £67.3M over the 2021/22 to 2025/26 period. The £20.0M allocated to Customer Services & E-Government significantly outpaces other departmental budgets, underscoring its central role in the council's financial planning and service delivery model.

To provide a clearer picture of how spending is distributed, the table below outlines the top ten departments by expenditure. This comparison highlights the scale of investment in customer-facing and digital services relative to other critical council functions.

Rank Department Amount % of Total
1 Customer Services & E-Government £20.0M 29.8%
2 Finance & Property £11.1M 16.5%
3 Regeneration £10.9M 16.2%
4 Operations £9.5M 14.2%
5 Corporate Management £8.1M 12.0%
6 Covid £2.1M 3.1%
7 Planning Services £1.3M 1.9%
8 Communities £1.2M 1.7%
9 Legal & Democratic Services £951K 1.4%
10 Non-Distributed Costs £704K 1.0%

The table clearly shows that Customer Services & E-Government's spend of £20.0M is substantially higher than the next largest departments. For instance, Finance & Property spent £11.1M, representing 16.5% of the total, and Regeneration spent £10.9M, or 16.2%. This indicates a deliberate strategic choice by Rossendale Borough Council to prioritise investment in its customer service infrastructure and digital capabilities. The difference between the top department and the second highest is almost £9M, underscoring the scale of this focus.

Contextualising Departmental Spending

The significant allocation to Customer Services & E-Government reflects the evolving demands on local authorities. Councils are increasingly expected to provide seamless, digital-first interactions for residents, alongside traditional services. An investment of £20.0M over five years suggests a long-term strategy to build and maintain robust systems capable of handling these demands. This includes everything from website development and maintenance to customer relationship management systems and staff training for digital tools.

While Customer Services & E-Government leads the spending, other departments also receive notable funding to deliver their specific functions. Operations, for example, spent £9.5M, accounting for 14.2% of the total, indicating significant investment in day-to-day service delivery. Corporate Management spent £8.1M, or 12.0%, which covers the overarching governance and administrative functions of the council. These figures, while smaller than the top department, are still substantial and essential for the council's overall operation.

The data provides a valuable insight into Rossendale Borough Council's financial priorities over the analysed period. The concentration of spending in Customer Services & E-Government highlights a clear strategic direction towards enhancing resident experience and modernising service delivery through digital means. This sustained investment is a key component of how the council plans to engage with and serve its community in the coming years.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Rossendale Borough Council about the specific projects and initiatives funded by the Customer Services & E-Government department's £20.0M spend.
  • Attend council committee meetings, which are open to the public, to hear discussions about departmental budgets and service delivery.
  • Check Rossendale Borough Council's published contracts register to see details of agreements made with suppliers for customer services and e-government projects.
  • Write to your local councillor about the significant investment in Customer Services & E-Government, asking how this spending benefits residents and improves local services.
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data for Rossendale Borough Council and other Lancashire councils yourself.
This article is based on Rossendale Borough Council's published spending data covering 2021/22 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
